bundle.shortDescription: Independent security research toolkit for HSFZ/UDS diagnostic protocols.
bundle.longDescription: BMSecResearch is an independent, open-source security research toolkit for the BMW diagnostic protocol stack (HSFZ over TCP/UDP, UDS per ISO 14229). Five integrated workflows: reverse-engineer captured flash sessions by extracting the underlying .bin payload from a Wireshark pcap; sniff a live HSFZ flash on a network interface and recover the image frame-by-frame; perform a bounded read of the MEVD17 unprotected calibration region (read-only, scope-enforced at compile time and runtime); stand up a stateful DME simulator that impersonates an MEVD17 ECU so diagnostic and flashing tools can be analyzed against a benign target instead of real hardware; and run an HSFZ man-in-the-middle proxy between a tester and a real or simulated DME for protocol research, including VIN-handling research. Every session is persisted to disk with timestamps and can be exported as a Wireshark-ready .pcap. Authorized security research and educational use on vehicles you own — see SCOPE.md and DISCLAIMER.md. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by BMW AG or BMW Group.
